actionUnits.condition: Verification step. This GameObject has a BoxCollider (enabled, non-trigger) and no Rigidbody; thus only Trigger actions are valid. The attached MonoBehaviour is Fire (m_Script guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d), and the scene MonoBehaviour settings match the script’s serialized fields: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60 (note Awake will override timeToOn to a random value in this range), downMultiplier=2, onNeighborsThreshold=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, mainEffectGo fileID=1849464941111748095. No UnityEvent m_Calls are configured on this component, so there are no automatic UnityEvent-based triggers; only code-driven state changes via Update/WaterHit/neighbor notifications.

Ignition via neighbor influence. Precondition required: populate this Fire.neighbors with at least 2 Fire neighbors within maxNeighborDistance (4) and ensure at least one neighbor is sufficiently lower in Y by > minNeighborDownDistance (3) to set hasDownNeighbor=true. Either: (A) call FindNeighbors(allFires, fireId) to auto-fill neighbors and count on neighbors, or (B) manually set neighbors via inspector and call NotifyNeighborState(true) twice to reach onNeighborsThreshold. With onNeighbors >= 2, shouldTurnOn will be set true. Then wait > timeToOn (Awake random in [40,60]) to allow Update() to flip isOn to true and call UpdateFireState(true), which plays the particle system and notifies neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(1).

Water suppression, phase 1. Precondition: fire is currently ON from the previous step. During this 1.9s window, an external water system must repeatedly call IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(normal) on this component (any normal is acceptable) to start turning off. Each WaterHit should reset currentHitInterval to 0 and keep isTurningOff=true while accumulating currentHitTime. Do not allow >2s between hits (maxHitInterval=2).
Water suppression, phase 2. Continue applying water hits (call WaterHit within <=2s intervals) to accumulate totalHitTime past 5s. After this step, cumulative currentHitTime should be >= 3.8s.

Water suppression, phase 3. Continue WaterHit within <=2s intervals. When currentHitTime >= totalHitTime (5), Update() will turn the fire OFF: isOn=false, shouldTurnOn=false, timeToOnDelta=0, isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime=0; UpdateFireState(true) will be invoked, stopping the particle system and notifying neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1).

Hit interval timeout behavior check. If the fire is ON and isTurningOff was true but WaterHit is not called for >= maxHitInterval (2s), Update() should reset isTurningOff=false and currentHitTime=0 without turning the fire OFF. This step validates that branch. Precondition for this step: re-enable the fire (either via the neighbor ignition step again or by manually calling SetIsOn(true)) and perform WaterHit once, then wait >=2.1s with no further hits.

Direct setter path (no neighbor notifications). Manually call SetIsOn(true) to verify UpdateFireState(false) branch (no neighbor notifications, just particle system state change). Then call SetIsOn(false) to verify turning off via the same branch. This requires the script component fileID to be callable and supports methods with parameters.
